topic: Validate the deep-loop-workflows plus deep-loop-runtime merge design before system-deep-loop execution

focus: Baseline topology, scope, and current contracts
noveltyJustification: First pass established the requested read-only research scope, current two-skill topology, and direct-artifact validation boundary.
keyQuestions: What is in scope for this research lineage?
What is the current deep-loop topology?
Can direct lineage artifacts be mechanically validated without resolver writes?
answeredQuestions: What is in scope for this research lineage?
What is the current deep-loop topology?
Can direct lineage artifacts be mechanically validated without resolver writes?

findingDetails.title: Reference phase is explicitly read-only and scoped to synthesis artifacts
Current topology is still a public workflow hub plus frozen runtime backend
The child-002 plan already separates irreversible move, path repair, metadata consolidation, and validation gates
The direct verifier accepts artifact-dir input and checks narrative, route-proof state, and delta records only under that artifact dir

graphEvents.label: What is the safe scope for this lineage?
Reference research is read-only and artifact-scoped
Current topology is hub plus runtime

focus: Structural move and bidirectional path-coupling stress test
noveltyJustification: Second pass added concrete risk decomposition for forward coupling, reverse coupling, system-spec-kit tooling-borrow, and metadata consolidation.
keyQuestions: Does the structural merge plan cover both path-coupling directions?
What validation must run before child 002 can claim done?
answeredQuestions: Does the structural merge plan cover both path-coupling directions?
What validation must run before child 002 can claim done?

findingDetails.title: Forward runtime-to-workflows coupling is explicitly mapped and mostly preserves hop count while deleting the old segment
Reverse workflows-to-runtime coupling needs a different hop-count rule and is not safe as a blanket string rewrite
The system-spec-kit tooling-borrow path repair is load-bearing and correctly kept in child 002 rather than deferred
The planned verification stack matches the failure modes: package validation, graph-metadata count, runtime tests, typecheck, council tests, git-follow checks, and a live short run

graphEvents.label: Does child 002 cover structural move risks?

focus: External reference migration and advisor/routing blast radius
noveltyJustification: Third pass expanded beyond command YAML into advisor projections, tests, agents, system-spec-kit integrations, and sk-doc examples.
keyQuestions: Is child 003 broad enough for non-code references and generated routing projections?
Which migration stages are most likely to leave stale old-skill names?
answeredQuestions: Is child 003 broad enough for non-code references and generated routing projections?
Which migration stages are most likely to leave stale old-skill names?

findingDetails.title: Reference migration must be dependency ordered; no safe moved-but-unrewritten intermediate state exists
Advisor code, generated projections, hardcoded tests, and divergence ledgers are migration-critical, not prose cleanup
Command contract migration spans source YAML, router markdown, and regenerated compiled outputs
System-spec-kit remains a high-risk integration consumer through package scripts, vitest globs, docs, and tests
Grandfather examples and parent-skill docs need semantic updates because system-deep-loop introduces a prefix exception, not just a string replacement

graphEvents.label: Does child 003 cover the external migration blast radius?
Advisor and generated projections are migration-critical
System-spec-kit is a high-risk integration consumer

focus: Fallback-router wiring, GLM-to-MiMo risk, and stop decision
noveltyJustification: Fourth pass mainly confirmed previous risks and added the concrete fallback-router gap; all key questions now have source-backed answers.
keyQuestions: Is automatic GLM-5.2 to MiMo-v2.5-Pro fallback already wired?
Should fallback wiring block the structural merge?
Has the research reached legal convergence?
answeredQuestions: Is automatic GLM-5.2 to MiMo-v2.5-Pro fallback already wired?
Should fallback wiring block the structural merge?
Has the research reached legal convergence?

findingDetails.title: The fallback router module is implemented and validates missing targets, cycles, scopes, hop count, approval, and same-pool rejection
Fanout retry exhaustion currently settles failure instead of resolving a fallback target
The live model registry has both GLM and MiMo active in separate pools, but both fallback_target fields are null
Fallback wiring is correctly tracked as optional and should not block child 002 or 003 unless the operator changes scope

ruledOut.approach: Treat GLM-to-MiMo fallback as already active during fan-out
ruledOut.reason: The registry has null fallback targets and fanout-pool settles retry-exhausted failures without calling resolveFallback.

ruledOut.approach: Block the structural rename on fallback wiring
ruledOut.reason: The fallback plan is explicitly optional and awaits an operator scope decision; structural and reference migration plans already account for current retry-and-salvage behavior.

graphEvents.label: Fanout retry exhaustion does not resolve fallback
